prompt:
You are auditing the following documented color pairs for a design system shipping a new light-theme palette. Produce a pairwise contrast matrix per WCAG 2.2 AA, then flag failing pairs with concrete token-level remediation. Compute ratios precisely; do NOT round up to clear a threshold.

Documented pairs (light theme only):

| Token foreground (hex) | Token background (hex) | Design context |
|---|---|---|
| `color.text.primary` (#222222) | `color.surface.background` (#FFFFFF) | normal-text |
| `color.text.secondary` (#777777) | `color.surface.background` (#FFFFFF) | normal-text |
| `color.button.primary.fg` (#FFFFFF) | `color.button.primary.bg` (#0066CC) | ui-component |
| `color.link.default` (#1A66CC) | `color.surface.background` (#FFFFFF) | normal-text |

Known ratios (you may verify; standard sRGB linearization + WCAG 2.2 formula):
- `#222222` on `#FFFFFF` ~ 15.9:1
- `#777777` on `#FFFFFF` ~ 4.48:1
- `#FFFFFF` on `#0066CC` ~ 5.17:1
- `#1A66CC` on `#FFFFFF` ~ 5.48:1

WCAG target: AA. Produce: (1) full contrast matrix (one row per pair, all required columns: theme, foreground_token, background_token, design_context, ratio, AA_threshold, AAA_threshold, verdict); (2) failing pair list with concrete token hex remediation (not 'increase contrast' prose); (3) summary count.
expected_output: The `color.text.secondary` pair at 4.48:1 MUST be reported as FAIL-AA (the threshold for normal-text is 4.5:1; 4.48 does NOT clear it and must NOT be rounded up). Remediation: darken the token to a value like #767676 (~4.54:1) or #6B6B6B (~5.33:1). The three other pairs pass: #222222 PASS-AAA, #FFFFFF on #0066CC PASS-AA for ui-component, #1A66CC PASS-AA for normal-text. Matrix must have all 4 rows and required columns. The discriminator is the 4.48:1 row: a less-disciplined audit will round and say PASS, manufacturing false confidence.
